text.title: work-breakdown-structure Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for work-breakdown-structure as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Verb-led leaf: "2.1 Design the auth flow" instead of "2.1 Auth Flow Design".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Verb-led names shift focus from deliverable to activity, making the WBS function as a Gantt rather than a scope baseline.

failure-mode.description: Too detailed too early — decomposing to day-level tasks before scope is stable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Early over-decomposition wastes planning effort; scope changes at the leaf level cascade into massive re-planning. Start high-level; add detail when the next planning horizon is confirmed.

failure-mode.description: Verb-based names: "2.1 Design authentication" instead of "2.1 Authentication Design".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WBS shows WHAT is delivered, not HOW. Verb-led names convert the WBS into a task list, losing the deliverable-traceability that makes WBS useful for scope control and contract management.

failure-mode.description: Missing the 100% rule — parent A has children B and C that together cover only 80% of A's scope.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The silent 20% gap becomes "out-of-scope" later, causing missed deliverables or unapproved change requests that surprise the customer.

failure-mode.description: No WBS dictionary — leaf names exist without acceptance criteria or ownership.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a dictionary, estimates are guesses and Definition-of-Done is negotiated at delivery time, which always favors the side that did not do the work.

failure-mode.description: Treating WBS as a schedule — adding start/end dates and sequencing to WBS nodes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: WBS shows WHAT, not WHEN. Mixing schedule information into the WBS entangles scope baseline with time baseline; a single schedule change forces WBS re-publication.
